prompt: Extract the inline error handling in the HTTP handlers into a middleware that catches panics and returns proper error responses

turns.content:
<task>
Extract the inline error handling in the HTTP handlers into a middleware that catches panics and returns proper error responses
</task>

arguments.reasoning: Each handler has an identical defer/recover block. I should extract this into a RecoveryMiddleware that wraps any http.HandlerFunc. Then the handlers can be simplified to just the business logic.

arguments.summary: All 3 handlers have identical 5-line defer/recover blocks. Will extract into a recovery middleware and simplify handlers.

next_steps.description: Create internal/middleware/recovery.go with Recovery(next http.HandlerFunc) http.HandlerFunc that wraps handler with defer/recover
Remove defer/recover blocks from all 3 handlers in handler.go
Build and test to verify behavior is unchanged

arguments.summary: Extracted duplicate defer/recover blocks into Recovery middleware in internal/middleware/recovery.go. Simplified all 3 handlers to pure business logic (removed 15 lines of boilerplate). Builds and tests pass.
arguments.final_answer:
Refactored panic recovery into middleware:
- Created internal/middleware/recovery.go with Recovery() wrapper
- Removed identical 5-line defer/recover blocks from all 3 handlers
- Handlers reduced to pure business logic
- Recovery middleware logs method/path with panic details
- All tests pass
